title: Should we count the three Reis as separate characters
display_title: Should we count the three Reis as separate characters
wikitext:
[]: Should we count the three Reis as separate characters?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Mon May 23, 2011 12:11 pm A lot of people keep saying that Rei's character development comes to a screeching halt after the Armisael disaster, but that's because she's DEAD. The Rei we know through most of the series and the Rei we know in 24-26' are two different people entirely. Rei III only has a fraction of the second's memories and none of the development and humanity that No. 2 had. Rei I was a normal little girl until Naoko killed her, and had none of the emotionlessness that the later ones had. Now, my question is, should we count Rei I, II, and three as separate people? They share portions of their memories, and have the same appearance, but they're really just different girls with similar characteristics when you think about it. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Mon May 23, 2011 12:18 pm Not sure, aren't they in fact the same? (but in different bodies)

[]:             Quote Post by LoCooLaidman » Mon May 23, 2011 12:21 pm I do, it's easier that way and it makes more sense. It's like having three diffrent characters using the same body (eh whatever), except for Rei I who is shorter (and evil). (What about Rei IIII... you know, the one that showed up in Sergeant Frog... oh... that's non-canon and poo)

[]: Re: Should we count the three Reis as separate characters?             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Mon May 23, 2011 12:27 pm CyberXIII wrote: A lot of people keep saying that Rei's character development comes to a screeching halt after the Armisael disaster, but that's because she's DEAD. The Rei we know through most of the series and the Rei we know in 24-26' are two different people entirely. Rei III only has a fraction of the second's memories and none of the development and humanity that No. 2 had. Rei I was a normal little girl until Naoko killed her, and had none of the emotionlessness that the later ones had. Now, my question is, should we count Rei I, II, and three as separate people? They share portions of their memories, and have the same appearance, but they're really just different girls with similar characteristics when you think about it. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon May 23, 2011 12:28 pm ....

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon May 23, 2011 12:35 pm It's important to note that the three Reis that we meet have distinct personalities, if only by a minimal degree. While they are the same entity each time, they also behave differently each time (due to the fragmentation). If observing the character development of Rei as a whole treat her as a single character; if observing her personalities, however, distinguish between the three (however, the default, if you should happen to not make the distinction, will always be Rei II).

[]: Re: Should we count the three Reis as separate characters?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon May 23, 2011 12:35 pm CyberXIII wrote: Rei III only has a fraction of the second's memories and none of the development and humanity that No. 2 had.

[]: Re: Should we count the three Reis as separate characters?             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon May 23, 2011 12:43 pm ...

[]: Re: Should we count the three Reis as separate characters?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon May 23, 2011 1:00 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: IIRC, they only show up as 'three characters' to the one person that knew her as all three... Gendo Ikari. I.E., it's the Rei Ayanami/s in his mind. Every other envisionment and manifestation of Rei in the film is the same... the current incarnation.

[]: Re: Should we count the three Reis as separate characters?             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon May 23, 2011 1:04 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 23, 2011 1:07 pm I see the ep 25 bit as Rei III reaching an identity, but it's not them all merging together, the first two are sort of dead and this inner monologue takes place before instrumentality. There's a huge title card telling us when it starts. (I see more of a split there. Rei II's mindset is explained and then Rei III basically goes: Uh, actually no, I think dying is awesome, so I'm not afraid of that "dark presence" (Lillith) and hate Gendo, unlike the chick that came before) but the process of identity building itself. Rei I would represent the "nature" and Rei II (who amassed most of the Memories, as some DVD commentary points it out) the "nurture" aspets. Rei III sort of represents A person's own decisions here (which EVA always puts an emphasis on, how the individual's choices decide as much as the outer happenings...) She talks about her indentity, about what makes her who she is, the doubts she had.... The point of it that even with the same memories and the same body, Rei III is notably different. As she herself puts it "We're all "Ayanami Rei" because the others call me that.", they even sound a little different in the ep 25 monologue - Rei III sounds a bit "drier". At least director's cut also consistently draws Rei III's eyes as more narrow/half-closed, which can't possibly be an actual difference in appearance (clones and stuff) but most likely is this anime-character-personality-eyesize thing. In addition to that, EoE has an aparition of Rei II turn up, pick up Gendo's glasses and hold them close to her, just after no III had basically told the guy to go screw himself. Also, the personalities are very different.... They only share that superficial coolness, but Rei I actually has expressions all the time, if subtle ones, and seems to have no problem with red, we all knew no II's very well fleshed-out personality up to hobbies, likes and dislikes and philosophic opinions (She seems to believe that ppl are mostly shaped by their surroundings (5, 14, referenced in 25) and is seen wondering whether there is a God. ("What are humans? the creations of god? The creations of men and women?" etc)) and then there's no III. Ppl tend to say she's more human, but to me, she always felt like a vengeful ghost... Can't call her an empty shell because you see her thinking about her purpose , but she's pretty close. she has some hate for Gendo in one Corner and some residual feelings for Shinji in the other, some deathwish here and there, but mostly emptiness. She'll just calmly stand next to that guy she hates most of the time. As for the Memories, I think I've recently read something that confirmed that the tube sessions backup them, so Rei III would remember almost everything up until shortly before her death, there were a few days lost at worst...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon May 23, 2011 1:08 pm I agree they should be treated as different people... and in fact think you aren't going far enough. I'd argue that every time someone's soul is split, recombined or merged with others that person has been fundamantally changed. Thus: Lilith splits into Lilith fragment and Rei 1 Rei 1 splits into Rei 2 and unit 00 Rei 2, unit 00 (and possibly Armisarael and other angels?) combine into GNR 1 GNR 1 salvaged into Rei 3 Rei 3 absorbs Adam/Kaworu Rei 3a merges with Lilith fragment into GNR 2 GNR 2 merges with Yui/01 and Shinji GNR 2a releases the Rei-tachi GNR 2b merges with EVERYONE GNR 2c reverts to GNR 2, releasing everyone else absorbed in the meantime GNR 2 breaks apart, with at least one portion of the soul remaining active as 'Bookend Rei'.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Mon May 23, 2011 1:17 pm I think we should let Rei tell us what she thinks. Episode 25 wrote: Rei III: Who am I? Rei II: You are Rei Ayanami. Rei III: But who are you? Are you Rei Ayanami as well? Rei II: Correct. I am a thing that is recognized as Rei Ayanami. Rei I: We are all things that are recognized as Rei Ayanami. Rei III: But how can all of us possibly be me? Rei II: It is simply because the others call us Rei Ayanami. That is the only reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon May 23, 2011 1:22 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 23, 2011 1:22 pm A lot of "dead" people showed up. Some Echo of sorts is beaing carried over, since Rei III was left witch some sad remainder of no II's feelings for Shinji and the last words no I ever heard seem to have engrained themselves deeply into no II's mind. The dialogue from ep 25 suggest that Lillith's "self" esists as a dark presence within her, maybe it's a similar thing with 'em predecessors. We do see all three of them standing next to each other, after all, and they're clearly differenciated (No II wears clothes. perhaps clothes = humanity/indentity...?) Afterwards, it may as well be a composite personality which also includes Lillith - everything is a bit muddy in the endings, both versions drop the narrative to some degree to get to the "important" stuff. Some of these instances are also shown from Shinji's perspective, and he was too shocked with the whole clone thing to wonder whether they're different people...

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon May 23, 2011 1:36 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 23, 2011 1:39 pm The trio shows up after Gendo kicks the bucket in whatever way, the phantom Yui's no longer there.... - also, there are other instances of characters not tanging all at once - Maya's hand, that random arm in the corridors... It all gets a bit muddy with instrumentality, but... any idiot should be able to tell they're different, just... watch 'em episodes... Or duh, there really aren't too much definite answers, it may be some of that stuff that was left intentionally vague. But think about the discrepancy here... that apparition of Rei II just picks up the glasses after her sucessor just shattered the last ones...

[]: Re: Should we count the three Reis as separate characters?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon May 23, 2011 1:42 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: First off, the Gendo scene is a complete mess. We see him standing headless, we see him not become LCL, we see Eva-01 there for chrissakes. It's a completely surreal scene, almost moreso than any other in the series and that's saying something. The fact that Rei 3 uses quantum magic or whatever to go back and get people as well as do the ep 01 bookend has no real bearing here, as do most of the details you bring up here.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 23, 2011 1:44 pm Also, the script for 25 explicitly numbers them... @the wayniac: Interesting, I never noticed the shirt change. Still, I always saw the monstrous EVA 01 as standing for Shinji in a way... wasn't there a statement on that? I read it on TV tropes, tough, so it could as well be complete bull.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon May 23, 2011 1:52 pm Kendrix wrote: Also, the script for 25 explicitly numbers them... @the wayniac: Interesting, I never noticed the shirt change. Still, I always saw the monstrous EVA 01 as standing for Shinji in a way... wasn't there a statement on that? I read it on TV tropes, tough, so it could as well be complete bull.

Article: Should we count the three Reis as separate characters
 have no problem with red, we all knew no II's very well fleshed-out personality up to hobbies, likes and dislikes and philosophic opinions (She seems to believe that ppl are mostly shaped by their surroundings (5, 14, referenced in 25) and is seen wondering whether there is a God. ("What are humans? the creations of god? The creations of men and women?" etc)) and then there's no III. Ppl tend to say she's more human, but to me, she always felt like a vengeful ghost... Can't call her an empty shell because you see her thinking about her purpose , but she's pretty close. she has some hate for Gendo in one Corner and some residual feelings for Shinji in the other, some deathwish here and there, but mostly emptiness. She'll just calmly stand next to that guy she hates most of the time. As for the Memories, I think I've recently read something that confirmed that the tube sessions backup them, so Rei III would remember almost everything up until shortly before her death, there were a few days lost at worst...

I'd argue that every time someone's soul is split, recombined or merged with others that person has been fundamantally changed. Thus: Lilith splits into Lilith fragment and Rei 1 Rei 1 splits into Rei 2 and unit 00 Rei 2, unit 00 (and possibly Armisarael and other angels?) combine into GNR 1 GNR 1 salvaged into Rei 3 Rei 3 absorbs Adam/Kaworu Rei 3a merges with Lilith fragment into GNR 2 GNR 2 merges with Yui/01 and Shinji GNR 2a releases the Rei-tachi GNR 2b merges with EVERYONE GNR 2c reverts to GNR 2, releasing everyone else absorbed in the meantime GNR 2 breaks apart, with at least one portion of the soul remaining active as 'Bookend Rei'.
